name: Maxwell Kwadwo Baidoo
title: Development Leader, Agribusiness Professional and Natural Resources Advocate
location: Sefwi Humjibre, Western North Region, Ghana
intro: Maxwell Kwadwo Baidoo is a development-focused leader whose work connects youth opportunity, responsible natural-resource governance, education access and regional advocacy in Ghana's Western North Region.

stats.label: students recommended for full tuition scholarships at KAAF University
BECE candidates supported with mathematical sets and pens
appointed President of WENDA after distinguished service
peace campaigns led around national elections

biography: Maxwell hails from Sefwi Humjibre in the Bibiani-Anhwiaso-Bekwai Municipality of the Western North Region of Ghana. He began his basic education at Humjibre M/A Primary School and Humjibre Anglican Junior High School before studying General Science at Ghana Secondary Technical School in Takoradi.

He earned a Bachelor's degree in Natural Resources Management from Kwame Nkrumah University of Science and Technology in 2019, completed national service with the Forest Services Division in Bibiani, and later returned to KNUST for postgraduate studies in Agribusiness Management.

His leadership journey has included service with RENARSA-KNUST, NUSSA-KNUST, NASPA in Bibiani-Anhwiaso-Bekwai, NUSSA-National and WENDA, where he has advanced student outreach, career guidance, sanitation, scholarships, internships, employment pathways and regional development advocacy.

timeline.event: Admitted to KNUST to study Natural Resources Management.
Elected Vice President of NUSSA-KNUST, leading outreach and career guidance initiatives.
Completed undergraduate studies and served with the Forest Services Division in Bibiani.
Elected NASPA Vice President in Bibiani-Anhwiaso-Bekwai and appointed Alumni Coordinator for NUSSA-National.
Appointed General Secretary of WENDA, supporting scholarships, youth placements and development partnerships.
Returned to KNUST for postgraduate studies in Agribusiness Management.

impact.title: Scholarships and Student Access
impact.body: Through WENDA and strategic recommendations, Maxwell helped open scholarship pathways, including full tuition opportunities for about 30 students at KAAF University.

impact.title: Youth Leadership and Mentorship
impact.body: He continues to coach young people, especially young women, to take up leadership responsibilities and break barriers in their communities.

impact.title: Education Support
impact.body: As WENDA President, he led support for 500 BECE candidates through the donation of mathematical sets and pens across the Western North Region.

impact.title: Regional Advocacy
impact.body: His agenda includes advocacy for a university, a regional hospital, school infrastructure support and stronger partnerships across public, private and nonprofit actors.

services.title: Policy and Development Convening
services.description: Facilitating conversations across institutions, community leaders, youth groups and development partners.

services.title: Youth Opportunity Programs
services.description: Designing outreach, mentorship, internship and employability initiatives for students and graduates.

services.title: Natural Resources Advocacy
services.description: Promoting responsible resource governance and community awareness on illegal mining and environmental harm.

services.title: Agribusiness Insight
services.description: Connecting natural resources education with practical agribusiness strategy and regional livelihoods.
